reasoning: Without search tools to gather real-time data or recent market trends for AOS up to the October 26, 2025 cutoff, I rely strictly on base rates for large-cap US equities. Overall, stocks exhibit a slight upward drift over time due to the positive equity risk premium. Over very short horizons such as 7 days, the outcome is nearly a random walk (~51%). Over roughly one month, the probability of a gain is historically around 52%. Over a three-month horizon, it slightly increases to approximately 53-54%. The most recent quoted price doesn't change this base-rate assessment without further context.
